title: The Tiananmen Papers
workType: Compilation of selected Chinese official documents
description: The Tiananmen Papers is a compilation of selected chinese official documents by Compiled by Zhang Liang. Controversy about this book include authenticity of selected documents and selection bias.
descriptionParagraphs: The Tiananmen Papers is a compilation of selected chinese official documents by Compiled by Zhang Liang. Controversy about this book include authenticity of selected documents and selection bias.

The surviving record is interesting because it shows how even ordinary-looking books can acquire a charged political afterlife. As a compilation of selected chinese official documents, it can be read not only for subject matter but for the way form, tone, and circulation make a text feel dangerous, intimate, or politically usable to anxious officials.

It also matters as part of a wider censorship history in China. The present page is a dossier starter built from source-tracked ban records; the surviving note currently says: Controversy about this book include authenticity of selected documents and selection bias. More publication history, translations, and close reading can be added later.

banningParagraphs: The Tiananmen Papers entered censorship debates as a compilation of selected chinese official documents associated with controversy, publication history, and state scrutiny. In the current dossier, the main state objections cluster around political sensitivity.

The earliest event currently captured here is 20th-21st century in China, where Chinese state censors and party authorities banned or suppressed publication. Controversy about this book include authenticity of selected documents and selection bias. Controversy about this book include authenticity of selected documents and selection bias.

counterReadings.title: 100 Banned Books: Censorship Histories of World Literature
counterReadings.author: Nicholas J. Karolides, Margaret Bald, and Dawn B. Sova
counterReadings.note: A compact reference on how censorship systems moved across states, churches, and courts.

counterReadings.title: Banned Books: 387 B.C. to 1978 A.D.
counterReadings.author: Anne Lyon Haight
